If in an eclipse the beginning is on the eastern side and the

66 Brhat SamhitU
liberation on the western side of the Moon'sdisc, it is czWed jfarana
—Decaying. As a result of this people will be troubled by hunger
and threat of weapons and consequently be utterly helpless.
[Cf. Kasyapa—
^ ll]

If the liberation starts from the middle of the Moon's disc
and if that part becomes bright at first, it is called Madhya vidarana
—cleaving in the middle. Its consequences would be disaffection
in the king's army, and sparse rains, but good crops.
[Cf. Kasyapa—■
tpn SpfTRft cRT I
■V Ni
In the light of Kasyapa's statement it would be better if we inter-
pret tjfrrers etc. in the text with the * as applicable to both the pre-
ceding word and the following one. Then it would mean—Neither
good crops nor good rains.
